summary: Iter 164 - verified BANK HOLIDAY banner cleanup primitives: (1) new POST /api/admin/marketing-studio/delete-hero-slide-by-text, (2) ?aggressive=true on cleanup-orphan-hero-slides, (3) _orphan_slides_cleanup_kickstart background task on boot, plus the red 'Delete slide by text' button in Marketing Studio header. 9/9 new pytest + 38/38+1 regression + live boot-kickstart test all pass.

minor.issue: logger.info('[orphan-slides] auto-cleaned %d ...') does not appear in /var/log/supervisor/backend.{out,err}.log even when the kickstart runs and deletes slides. Same pattern affects existing [gsc] and [visualizer] startup log lines, so this is a pre-existing logging-configuration issue (uvicorn installs logging handlers before server.py's logging.basicConfig() runs). Functionality is not affected - cleanup verifiably works. Purely an observability nit.

action_items: Optional: fix the startup logging so [orphan-slides] / [gsc] / [visualizer] INFO lines are captured in supervisor logs - gives operators audit trail of what was cleaned on each deploy. One-liner: move logging.basicConfig() to before uvicorn imports, or use uvicorn's log_config to enable the server-module logger. No user-facing regression.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/marketing_studio.py line 538: delete_one({'image': slide['image'], 'title': slide.get('title','')}) in cleanup_orphan_hero_slides relies on (image,title) being unique. If two legacy slides share the same image+title the loop deletes only one per iteration; the next slide will be hit on the next call. Minor - switching to delete by id if present would be more surgical.
server.py _orphan_slides_cleanup_kickstart reuses the same (image,title) deletion heuristic; same minor non-uniqueness concern.

delete_hero_slide_by_text scans title/badge/subtitle only - if an admin named their slide generically but put the BANK HOLIDAY text in an alt_text or link_url, it won't be caught. In practice this is fine; flagging for future extension.

server.py still has the self-assignment typo from iter 163: update['scheduled_start'] = update['scheduled_start'] = now_iso around line 561 of routes/marketing_studio.py (not in the iter 164 diff - carried over).
seed_data_creation: Each test inserts its own hero_slides and marketing_assets docs with PYTEST-/ITER164-prefixed identifiers and cleans them up in a finally block. No persistent data created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New test files: /app/backend/tests/test_marketing_studio_iter164.py (9 test cases, runs against live backend) and /app/backend/tests/test_iter164_boot_kickstart.py (standalone script - does 'sudo supervisorctl restart backend' so don't run during critical testing; sleeps 32s for kickstart). Frontend buttons live in /admin/marketing-studio header with testids marketing-studio-delete-slide-by-text-btn (red) and marketing-studio-cleanup-orphans-btn (amber). window.prompt default is pre-filled with 'BANK HOLIDAY'.

detailed_verification.pytest_iter164_new_tests: 9 passed in 7.60s - TestDeleteHeroSlideByText (auth required, <3 char rejection, empty rejection, case-insensitive title match, badge match, no-match empty response) + TestCleanupOrphansAggressive (default mode still works, aggressive accepted, aggressive removes published-asset image match Strategy-3)
detailed_verification.pytest_regression_suite: 38 passed, 1 skipped in 23.65s - matches expectation (test_marketing_studio_hero_slides + _schedule + _unpublish + 5 visualizer files)
detailed_verification.boot_kickstart_test: Injected untagged slide with image matching a deleted marketing_asset. Ran `sudo supervisorctl restart backend`, slept 32s, confirmed DB count == 0. Kickstart works. (Note: log line absent from supervisor logs - see minor issue above.)
detailed_verification.frontend_buttons_present: Both buttons visible in header. Delete-by-text button has red border class, orphan button has amber border class.
detailed_verification.frontend_prompt_prefill: window.prompt dialog fires with default_value='BANK HOLIDAY' - verified via page.on('dialog') listener.
detailed_verification.frontend_cancel_flow: Cancelling the prompt does nothing (no API call fired, no toast) - expected behaviour.
detailed_verification.aggressive_strategy_3_proof: test_aggressive_removes_image_match_even_if_published explicitly proves the new Strategy-3 behaviour: marketing_asset with deleted=False AND published_to='promo_banner' is protected from non-aggressive cleanup but removed under aggressive mode.
